question: What does Table 1.3-1 say about NuScale fuel assemblies, and how does Figure 5.1-1 show the core region within the module?

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether the design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch01_0056.title: Critical Heat Flux Testing - Preliminary Fuel Design
nuscale_ch01_0056.summary: This section outlines the verification and confirmation tests for unique NuScale Power Module design features, focusing on areas lacking prior data or operational experience. Specifically, it describes two critical heat flux (CHF) test programs for the fuel design: the preliminary program supported code development and safety analysis, providing data for the NSP2 CHF correlation used in the final fuel design.

nuscale_ch01_0733.title: SRP 15.4.1, Rev 3: Uncontrolled Control Rod Assembly Withdrawal From a Subcritical or Low Power Startup ConditionII.1.B Fuel Centerline Temperatures
nuscale_ch01_0733.summary: This section confirms that for SRP 15.4.1, Rev 3, regarding uncontrolled control rod assembly withdrawal from a subcritical or low power startup condition, NuScale ensures fuel centerline temperatures remain within safe limits by applying a heat generation rate limit during slower reactivity insertions. This approach ensures that fuel centerline melting limits are not exceeded, demonstrating conformance with regulatory criteria.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and nominal parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in core output, fuel assembly design, reactor coolant system, and safety systems, with detailed data provided in tables for side-by-side comparison. The comparison demonstrates NuScale's unique design choices, such as smaller size, fewer components, and simplified systems relative to conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0794.title: SRP 15.7.4, Rev 1: Radiological Consequences of Fuel Handling AccidentsII.5 Radiation Detection in Containment
nuscale_ch01_0794.summary: The NuScale design does not use ESF-grade ventilation systems for accident mitigation, relying instead on nonsafety-related ventilation that meets regulatory guidelines but is not credited in accident analyses. While the standard radiation detection criteria reference traditional containment buildings, NuScale’s design uses individual steel containment vessels for each module, so the intent of the criteria is applied to the Reactor Building where fuel handling occurs, rather than to a conventional containment structure.

nuscale_ch01_0037.title: Control Building
nuscale_ch01_0037.summary: This section describes the refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM), which are conducted independently for each module. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling area, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and inspection.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section explains that simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings are included in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It lists the types of diagrams provided, references tables and figures for diagram legends, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must supply site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest at the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

edges.description: Section A references regulatory guidance and conformance tables that set requirements for radiation protection and design features, while Section B describes system features (such as degasification and pressure boundary integrity) that are designed to satisfy those regulatory requirements.

Section A lists regulatory guidance and conformance statements relevant to radiation protection design features, while Section B describes testing programs (including for fuel design) that provide data to support the safety analyses required by those regulatory criteria, thus Section B supports the claims of regulatory conformance made in Section A.

Section A lists regulatory guidance documents and their conformance status for radiation protection design features, while Section B lists regulatory guidance and conformance for fuel behavior and safety system requirements, both referencing the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to demonstrate compliance with NRC criteria.

Section A references regulatory guidance and conformance tables that set requirements for radiation protection design features, while Section B provides a summary description of the reactor coolant system, which includes the core region and fuel assemblies that must comply with those regulatory requirements.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides nominal design values for NuScale fuel assemblies, which are referenced in Section A as part of the overall regulatory conformance discussion, connecting specific design parameters to regulatory criteria.

Section B describes testing programs that provide data to support the safety analysis discussed in Section A, specifically verifying that unique design features (such as those in the RCS described in Section A) meet safety requirements.

Section A describes the reactor coolant system's features for maintaining pressure boundary integrity, which is a prerequisite for the fuel centerline temperature analyses and regulatory conformance discussed in Section B.
Section A provides specific operational details (e.g., degasification and venting functions) that elaborate on the general system components and functions described in Section B.

Section B (Table 1.3-1) provides nominal design values for NuScale fuel assemblies for comparison with typical PWRs, while Section A references Figure 5.1-1, which visually depicts the core region within the module that contains these fuel assemblies, thus connecting the tabulated data to its physical layout.

Section A describes the testing programs for NuScale fuel design features that provide data supporting safety analyses, while Section B lists regulatory criteria and conformance statements that these analyses and test results must satisfy.

Section A discusses testing programs that verify unique design features of the NuScale fuel assemblies, while Section B provides a summary description of the reactor coolant system, which includes the core region where these fuel assemblies are located, thus connecting the verification of fuel design (A) to its placement and function within the core region (B).
Table 1.3-1 in Section B provides nominal design values for NuScale fuel assemblies, which are the basis for the verification and confirmation tests described in Section A.

Section A references regulatory criteria and conformance related to fuel centerline temperatures and system responses, while Section B provides a summary description of the reactor coolant system design, which includes the core region and its components, thus Section B specifies the physical and functional context for the regulatory requirements discussed in Section A.

Section B (Table 1.3-1) provides nominal design values for NuScale fuel assemblies, which Section A references as part of the regulatory analysis to ensure that fuel centerline temperatures and other safety criteria are met.

Section B's Table 1.3-1 provides nominal design values for NuScale fuel assemblies, which are referenced and further detailed in Section A's summary description of the reactor coolant system and its components.

Section A and Section B both discuss NuScale's conformance with regulatory guidance for radiological safety, with Section A focusing on accident mitigation systems and Section B detailing radiation protection design features and their alignment with specific regulatory guides.

Section A discusses the radiological consequences and containment strategies for fuel handling accidents, while Section B describes the reactor coolant system's design features that contribute to maintaining containment integrity, thus Section B provides supporting technical details relevant to the containment functions referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses the radiological safety systems and regulatory conformance for fuel handling accidents, while Section B outlines the testing programs (including critical heat flux testing) that provide technical data to support the safety analysis referenced in Section A, thus Section B supports the claims made in Section A regarding safety and regulatory compliance.

Section A describes operational procedures for refueling NuScale modules, while Section B lists regulatory conformance for radiation protection design features, with both sections connected through the need for refueling operations (A) to comply with radiation protection requirements (B) during such activities.

Section A describes the operational process of moving a NuScale Power Module (NPM) for refueling, while Section B outlines the reactor coolant system's (RCS) features that maintain system integrity during both normal and emergency operations, with both sections connected by their focus on the handling and safety of the reactor module during different operational states.

Section B describes testing programs that generate data supporting the safety analysis of NuScale fuel assemblies, which are summarized in Table 1.3-1, while Figure 5.1-1 (referenced in the analysis question) visually depicts the core region where these fuel assemblies are located, thus connecting the technical data (Section B) to its physical context (Figure 5.1-1).

Section A describes the use of figures and tables (such as Table 1.3-1) to provide visual clarification of design information, which supports the regulatory conformance discussions in Section B by supplying detailed design documentation referenced in regulatory reviews.

Section A provides visual and schematic details (including figures and tables) that clarify and support the system descriptions found in Section B, such as the layout and function of reactor components like the core region and fuel assemblies.

Section A provides visual and schematic details (including figures and tables) that clarify the design of NuScale components, while Section B describes the testing programs that generate data to support and verify those design features, connecting the visualized design with its technical validation.

Section A describes general plant features and utility structures, while Section B details the reactor coolant system's function and integrity, with both sections providing context for understanding how plant infrastructure (A) supports and interfaces with core systems (B) relevant to the analysis of fuel assemblies and core regions.

Section B describes testing programs that provide data supporting the safety analysis of unique NuScale fuel assembly design features, which are referenced in Section A's general plant description as part of the plant's features of special interest.

Section A provides the detailed drawings and figures (such as Figure 5.1-1) that visually clarify the design information referenced in Section B's regulatory conformance tables, thereby supporting the demonstration that the NuScale fuel assemblies and core region meet the radiation protection design features required by DSRS 12.3-12.4.

Section A provides visual and detailed design information, such as drawings and figures (including Figure 5.1-1), that clarify and specify the system descriptions and functional summaries presented in Section B.

Section A provides visual and schematic details (such as drawings and figures) that clarify the physical layout and design of NuScale components, while Section B describes the testing programs that verify and confirm the performance and safety of those same design features, connecting the visual information to the technical validation process.

Section A discusses the radiological consequences and containment measures during fuel handling accidents, while Section B describes the physical process and movement of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) during refueling operations, both addressing different aspects of fuel handling safety and logistics.

Section A discusses the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequences and containment systems, while Section B explains that drawings and tables (such as Table 1.3-1) provide detailed design information, indicating that the detailed data in Section B supports the regulatory analysis in Section A.
Section B provides visual and tabular details, such as Table 1.3-1 and Figure 5.1-1, that clarify and support the design information and regulatory conformance discussed in Section A.
Section B provides drawings and figures that visually clarify and supplement the operational descriptions found in Section A, such as the movement and positioning of the NPM during refueling.

Section A and Section B both introduce the use of figures and tables (such as Table 1.3-1 and Figure 5.1-1) to visually clarify design information in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, indicating that these visual aids are intended to supplement the written text for better understanding of components like fuel assemblies and core regions.

Section A provides a general description of plant features and structures, while Section B explains that figures and tables (such as Table 1.3-1 and Figure 5.1-1) are used to visually clarify and supplement the written design information described in sections like A.
